content.problemStatement:
In previous module, you've learned what Git is and how it works behind the scenes. \
Now it’s time to **actually start using Git**.

Imagine you’re starting a new project. \
To let Git track it -
**Open your terminal** and type:
```sss
git init
```
You’ve just created your **first Git repository**!

---

**What is a Git Repository?** \
A Git repository (repo) is where Git stores everything about your project. It :
- Keeps all versions of your files
- Lets you go back to older versions
- Helps you track changes safely
- Makes easier to collaborate with others without losing work.

---

When you run `git init`, Git creates a **hidden folder** called  **`.git`** — this is where all your project’s history and data are stored. \
That’s what turns your folder into a **Git repository**.
